name: Regulatory and Interoperability
summary: Regulatory audit, emergency access, legal retention, digital-signature scaffold, interoperability package governance, and no-false-compliance posture.
invariants: Core remains sovereign for regulatory contracts and gate-required effects.
AACI, GOS, apps, providers, and construction tooling are not regulatory authorities.
Emergency, signature, audit, retention, and interoperability pathways must fail closed without required basis, scope, rationale, lawfulContext, and gate where applicable.
Scaffold signature/interoperability records are not real legal integrations.
allowedWork: Maintain regulatory, emergency, signature, retention, and interoperability scaffold contracts.
Add fail-closed validation tests and schema alignment.
Clarify docs to avoid compliance or certification overclaims.
forbiddenWork: Claim regulatory compliance, production certification, or final legal authority.
Claim real ICP-Brasil, RNDS, TISS, FHIR, or qualified signature integration without implementation and evidence.
Use construction tooling as regulatory authority.
Simulate legal-valid evidence or signature success.
knownGaps: Real qualified signature provider integration is absent.
Real regulatory and interoperability endpoint integrations are absent.
Production certification remains out of scope.
notes: This territory preserves explicit non-claims as much as implementation contracts.
Territory metadata does not create legal effect.
